templates.text:
November 5, 2025

Ruan

Dear Hiring Manager,

I appreciate you taking the time to read this. It seems I've graduated college at a difficult time and I'm sure you're inundated with resumes from people in my exact situation: a college degree with no direct professional experience. If I could go back and revisit my college years, I'd have looked for that all-important internship that would've provided me with at least some experience; but sadly, that ship has sailed. Therefore, it stands to reason that having a strong cover letter is perhaps the only differentiator between myself and the hundreds of other applicants. With that in mind, let me attempt to do just that.

Your job posting for Data Analyst appears to be a great fit for my skills.

I'm eager and willing to begin my career on the ground floor, where I can learn, grow, and contribute meaningfully from day one. I'm told that talent and determination are widely recognized in the professional world and I'm hoping to demonstrate those characteristics in this role; I only need someone to take a chance.

I'm confident in my ability to adapt quickly and demonstrate my value to the company in this role. I'm an avid learner and strong listener. My curiosity drives me to continuously expand my knowledge base, and I believe that mindset directly translates into an employee with great potential.

I would welcome the chance to bring my energy, adaptability, and problem-solving skills to your team. Thank you for considering my application, I look forward to the opportunity to discuss how I can contribute to your success. Not sure if I nailed this or not, but I really do appreciate your reading until the end. Thank you for doing so.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomsen
